title: Orianthera tortuosa
intro: Orianthera tortuosa, commonly known as the "twisted leaf plant," is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions like arthritis and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Orianthera tortuosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Orianthera tortuosa is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional remedies for its perceived benefits in reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being.
taxonomy: Orianthera tortuosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae. It is native to Western Australia. The genus Orianthera is part of the subfamily Hemerocallidoideae.
